column1: As with all new generations of games
column2: Black & White bring a plethora of Pokémon to the fold. These Pokémon make up for the biggest amount of new Pokémon in a generation. This page will detail the stats of these 156 Pokémon. The base stats ath the moment are preliminary so they may change

column1: Recovery Items
In battle
column2: your Pokémon are going to get injured or use up all the power points. As such
column3: if you are in-battle or far from a Pokémon Centre
column4: you're going to need these Recovery Items. These items work in many ways and can heal your Pokémon's Hit Points
column5: restore them from a status condition or event bring a fainted Pokémon back.

Battle Effect Items
In battle
column2: you may wish to temporarily boost your Pokémon's stats. This can be done by using the below items
column3: which each boost the Pokémon's stats while they are in battle. These items are all one-use only but you can find them in a variety of places.

Hold Items
In the games
column2: there are a variety of items that give your Pokémon added perks if they have these items attached. Each item has different effects
column3: some affecting the Pokémon's status condition
column4: some boosting stats and some powering up moves. Some of these items are gone if used in battle
column5: while others will remain now matter how many battles the Pokémon is in.

column3: with the recent limit being that the Vitamins only add to the first 100 Effort Values in the stat. All vitamins are one-use only and they all slightly boost the Pokémon's happiness.

PokéBalls

PokéBalls are one of the most vital items that a Pokémon trainer can have. These items are used to capture the Pokémon. Each PokéBall has a different special effect that can help make capturing more likely or even give special bonuses to any Pokémon caught within that particular ball.

Mail
If you are trading a Pokémon to a friend and you wish to give them a message
column2: you have the ability of attaching a mail to the Pokémon. The mail come in a variety of styles. In the original Gold & Silver games
column3: you could manually write the mail and place anything you wanted on it. However
column5: it got restricted to to a massive selection set phrases. The mail is all one-use only and if you remove it from a Pokémon
column6: the mail gets sent to your in-game PC.

Fossils
In the games
column2: you may find one of several fossils of long extinct Pokémon. These fossils can be taken to specific plates
column4: Pewter City & Oreburgh Mining Museums as well as Devon Corp.
column5: where they will be revived into the Pokémon that became extinct.

Key Items
Key Items are a very varied and complex batch of items. These items are usually story based and can be used at specific points in the game. Some items give you access to certain areas
column2: some cause certain Pokémon to appear and some just allow you to hold and check other items. These items cannot be given to Pokémon to hold and you can only have one of each item.
